advisory.title: ASA-2025-002: Malicious peer can stall network by disseminating seemingly valid block parts
advisory.description: CometBFT advisory ASA-2025-002 covers missing validation that Part.Index equals Part.Proof.Index in block-part dissemination. A malicious peer can submit parts with mismatched indices, causing peers to disseminate invalid data and block reception of correct parts, potentially stalling the network. The v0.38.16..v0.38.17 patch (commit 415c0da22) modifies types/part_set.go to verify Part.Index matches Part.Proof.Index. Affects <=v0.38.16 and v1.0.0; fixed in v0.38.17 and v1.0.1.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.38.16 release commit, the last vulnerable v0.38 release before the v0.38.17 patch. The introducing commit fb91ef74 ("validate reactor messages", 2018-11-01) added Part.ValidateBasic in types/part_set.go without checking that Part.Index equals Part.Proof.Index; the v0.38.17 patch extends that same function with the missing equality check. Confidence is high because the baseline is the parent of the incomplete validation implementation and the patch targets exactly that validation gap.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag missing validation in types/part_set.go that Part.Index equals Part.Proof.Index, allowing a malicious peer to disseminate parts with crafted index/proof mismatches and block reception of legitimate parts.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: ASA-2025-002 (GHSA-r3r4-g7hq-pq4f) was published on 2025-02-03 with patches in cometbft v0.38.17 and v1.0.1.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.38.16 release commit, the latest tag in the <=0.38.16 vulnerable range.

checks.name: introducing_commit_contains_vulnerable_pattern
checks.details: git show fb91ef74 adds Part.ValidateBasic with checks for negative Index and oversized Bytes but no Part.Index == Part.Proof.Index check; the v0.38.17 patch adds that missing validation.
